HERO_TITLE: Live Comments for News Sites That Actually Keep Up With the Story
HERO_SUBTITLE: Your readers want to react in real time. FastComments delivers instant, live commenting with a 30kb footprint, built to handle election nights, breaking news, and the traffic spikes that come with them.
PROBLEM_TITLE: Commenting on News Sites Is Broken. Readers Know It.
PROBLEM_P1: Most commenting systems were built for blogs, not newsrooms. They load slowly, drag down page performance, and treat a local city council story the same as a national election thread drawing thousands of simultaneous readers. The result is a comments section that either feels dead or collapses under its own weight, neither of which serves your audience or your editorial mission.
PROBLEM_P2: The cost of getting it wrong is measurable. Slow-loading comment widgets hurt Core Web Vitals, which directly impacts your search rankings and ad revenue. Toxic or spam-filled threads erode reader trust and create legal and reputational risk. Heavy third-party scripts inject trackers that conflict with your privacy obligations and undermine the editorial independence your readers expect. And every reader who abandons your comments section is a reader who takes their engagement, and their loyalty, to social media instead.
PROBLEM_P3: What news readers actually want is straightforward: they want to see comments appear in real time as a story develops, they want conversations that are moderated well enough to be worth reading, and they want to participate without surrendering their data to an advertising network. They want a comments section that respects both the story and the people reading it.
WHY_FC_TITLE: A Commenting System Built for the Speed of News
WHY_FC_P1: FastComments was engineered for exactly the conditions news sites face daily. Comments appear instantly for every reader on the page, with no reload, no polling delay, and no stale threads. When a story breaks and your audience surges to weigh in, the conversation keeps pace with events in real time. This is not a bolt-on feature. Live commenting is the foundation of how FastComments works.
WHY_FC_P2: During breaking news and major events like election nights, court rulings, and live sports, your traffic doesn't scale linearly, it spikes. FastComments handles <%- Number(usageStats.pageLoadCount).toLocaleString() %>+ page loads per year across its client base and supports millions of active comments without degradation. The infrastructure is built to absorb sudden, massive surges in concurrent readers and commenters. Your comments section stays responsive when it matters most.
WHY_FC_P3: Performance is not a secondary consideration. The entire FastComments widget ships at roughly 30 kilobytes, a fraction of what systems like Disqus load onto your pages. That difference is significant for news sites, where every kilobyte affects page load time, Core Web Vitals scores, and ultimately your position in search results. Faster pages mean better SEO, lower bounce rates, and more ad impressions. A lightweight commenting system for publishers is not a luxury. It is a competitive advantage.
WHY_FC_P4: Integration is built for newsroom workflows. SSO lets your subscribers comment under their existing accounts. Webhooks push comment data into your CMS, analytics platforms, or moderation dashboards. Support for 14 languages with automatic detection means your international readership participates without friction. Whether you run a single metro daily or a network of publications across markets, FastComments fits the architecture you already have.
MODERATION_TITLE: Moderation Tools Designed for Newsroom Volume
MODERATION_P1: News site moderation operates under constraints that most platforms never consider. A political story can generate hundreds of comments in minutes, a significant share of which may be inflammatory, off-topic, or outright spam. You need moderation that works at that velocity without requiring a staff of twenty people watching threads around the clock. A slow or clumsy moderation system does not just cost time. It lets toxic content sit in front of your readers and your advertisers.
MODERATION_P2: FastComments includes a machine learning spam classifier that catches automated spam and coordinated abuse before it reaches your audience. Beyond automated filtering, the moderation interface supports bulk actions like approving, rejecting, or flagging dozens of comments in seconds. Moderation groups let you assign different teams to different sections or topics. Your politics desk can moderate their threads independently from your sports editors, each with their own rules and escalation paths.
MODERATION_P3: The goal is civil discourse without silence. Heavy-handed moderation kills conversation. Absent moderation breeds toxicity. FastComments gives your team the precision tools to enforce community standards quickly while keeping the productive, substantive reader conversations that build audience loyalty and distinguish your publication from the noise of social media.
PRIVACY_TITLE: Privacy That Matches Your Editorial Standards
PRIVACY_P1: FastComments is fully GDPR compliant and does not track, profile, or sell your readers' data. There are no hidden advertising pixels. No behavioral fingerprinting. No third-party data brokers riding on your comment widget. Your readers interact with your content, not with a surveillance layer disguised as engagement software. Anonymous commenting is available for stories where source protection or reader safety matters.
PRIVACY_P2: For news organizations, this is not merely a compliance checkbox. Your credibility depends on the trust your readers place in you. When a commenting system harvests user data or injects trackers, it creates a conflict between your editorial mission and your technology stack. Readers and regulators notice. FastComments removes that conflict entirely. Your comments section is a reader engagement tool, nothing more, and that clarity strengthens the relationship between your publication and your audience.
SCALE_TITLE: Performance at the Scale News Demands
SCALE_P1: FastComments processes over <%- Number(usageStats.pageLoadCount).toLocaleString() %> page loads per year and supports millions of comments across its client base of more than <%- Number(Math.floor(usageStats.tenantCount / 100) * 100).toLocaleString() %> organizations. The infrastructure is purpose-built for high-concurrency environments where thousands of readers load the same story simultaneously. Response times remain consistent whether a thread has ten comments or ten thousand.
SCALE_P2: Traffic spikes are not an edge case for news sites. They are the normal operating condition during any significant story. FastComments does not throttle, queue, or degrade during surges. There are no per-pageview pricing traps that penalize you for having a successful story. When your audience grows, your commenting system should keep up without sending you an overage invoice. With both fixed and flexible pricing plans, your costs stay predictable even when your traffic doesn't.
MIGRATION_TITLE: Migrate From Disqus or WordPress Comments in Minutes
MIGRATION_P1: Switching commenting systems sounds disruptive. In practice, FastComments imports your existing comments from Disqus, WordPress, and other platforms with a straightforward migration tool. Your existing threads, usernames, and conversation history transfer intact. Readers return to find their past comments exactly where they left them, and the transition is seamless enough that most will not notice the change, only the improvement.
MIGRATION_P2: There is no extended parallel-run period and no engineering project required. The typical migration takes minutes, not weeks. Your development team adds a lightweight script tag, configures your preferences, and your comments section is live with full history preserved.
COMMUNITY_P1: A comments section is the starting point, not the destination. FastComments includes awards and badges that recognize your most thoughtful contributors, reactions beyond simple upvotes, and built-in surveys that let you gauge reader sentiment on the issues you cover. Direct messaging between users lets conversations continue beyond individual threads. These are the tools that turn one-time commenters into regular participants and regular participants into loyal subscribers.
COMMUNITY_P2: Reader engagement on news sites competes directly with social media for attention and habit formation. The publications that win that competition are the ones that give their audience a reason to come back to the source. Analytics built into FastComments show you which stories drive the most engagement, which commenters are most active, and how your community is growing over time.
CTA_TITLE: Your Readers Are Already Talking. Give Them a Better Place to Do It.
CTA_SUBTITLE: No page slowdowns, no privacy trade-offs, no scaling surprises. See what FastComments looks like on your site with a free trial, no credit card required.
